items.snippet: ```text frustrating 답답한, 짜증나는 + It’s really frustrating when no one helps with the cleaning. + It was so frustrating. I had to go all the way back up 15 floors
items.body_raw:
```text
frustrating

답답한, 짜증나는

+ It’s really frustrating when no one helps with the cleaning.

+ It was so frustrating. I had to go all the way back up 15 floors just to get my umbrella.

​

muggy

후덥지근한

+ It’s so muggy today. I don’t even want to move.

​

in my case → as for me

내 경우엔 (회화체에서는 as for me 가 더 자연스러워요!)

in my case 는 회의나 미팅 등 좀 더 격식있는 자리에서 활용해보세요

+ As for me, I usually do all the vacuuming.

​

in my defense

(내) 변명을 하자면

+ In my defense, I did the dishes last night.

​

I have a long commute.

출퇴근 시간이 길어요

+ I don’t have much time for chores because I have a long commute.

​

quote

인용하다 / 인용구 (명언 인용할 때)

+ I like that quote: “A tidy space is a tidy mind.”

​

~ 라고 하더라고요 할 때 they say ~ 혹은 I heard ~ 라는 표현 많이 써요!

+ They say successful people make their bed every morning.

+ I heard successful people always make their bed in the morning.

​

I give you that.

그건 인정

+ Okay, I give you that. You’re at least cleaning your own room.

​

zone out / space out

멍 때리다

+ I just zone out on the subway during my commute.

+ I usually space out on the subway when I’m commuting.

​

pass away → die

돌아가시다

+ Her grandma passed away last year.

+ My dog died last year.

제가 애완동물에는 pass away 보다는 die 라고 했는데

찾아보니까 요즘은 반려동물을 가족처럼 생각하는 사람이 많아서

반려동물한테도 pass away 라는 표현을 흔하게 쓰더라구요

​

얘기가 나온 김에, (반려)동물이 죽었을 때 사용할 수 있는 완곡한 표현들 함께 봐요!

+ (S)He’s gone.

+ We lost our dog last night. - 잃어버렸다 도 되지만 죽었다를 부드럽게 표현할 때도 쓰여요

+ crossed the rainbow bridge 라고 해서 우리말에서처럼 무지개다리를 건넜다고도 표현해요. 위의 두개는 사람/동물 모두 쓸 수 있고 이 표현은 사람말고 동물한테만 쓰이며, SNS나 추모글 등 애정을 담은 은유적 표현으로 사용됩니다

​

shed

(털 등을) 흘리다, 털갈이 하다

+ My dog sheds a lot, so I vacuum every day.

​

street dog → stray dog

길거리 개 → 유기견

+ We adopted a stray dog last year.

유기견, 유기묘는 stray 와 쓰세요!

​

wireless → cordless

무선 - 전원을 연결하는 선이 무선이라고 말할땐 wire 대신 cord

+ There’s so much dog hair everywhere. My cordless vacuum can’t keep up.

+ There’s so much dog hair everywhere. A cordless vacuum just doesn’t cut it.

충분하지 않다고 할 때 not enough 대신

can’t keep up 감당이 안 되다, 따라가지 못하다

doesn’t cut it 안 통하다, 충분하지 않다

요렇게도 한 번 활용해보세요

​

It doesn’t do its job.

제대로 작동을 안 해요, 제 몫(역할)을 못해요

+ This old vacuum doesn’t do its job anymore.

​

(random) stuff

잡동사니

+ There’s a lot of stuff on my desk.

​

make room

공간을 만들다

+ I need to make room for my new monitor.

​

decluttering

정리 정돈, 불필요한 것 버리기

+ I’ve been decluttering my closet these days.

​

That’s how much you don’t like doing the dishes?

그 정도로 설거지를 하기 싫은 거예요?

​

disposable sponge

1회용 스폰지

scrub (off)

문지르다 (문질러 없애다)

+ I scrub the toilet with disposable sponges, and I really hate it.

​

After I shower, I have to wipe off the water using the squeegee. My mom gets mad at me if I don’t.

샤워하고 나면 스퀴지로 물기 제거해야돼요. 안하면 엄마가 화내요

​

refreshed

상쾌해진

+ I always feel refreshed after cleaning my desk.

​

doable

할 수 있는, 가능한

+ Deep cleaning sounds hard, but it’s doable if you take it step by step.

​

in your upbringing

성장 과정에서

+ Was cleaning a big deal in your upbringing?

​

shine shoes / polish shoes

구두 닦다

+ My dad used to give me some pocket money for shinining my dad’s shoes.

​

deep cleaning

대청소

+ I usually do a deep cleaning once a month.

​

mop

걸레질하다

+ I mop the floor about once a month.

​

food waste / food compost

음식물 쓰레기 / 음식물 퇴비

- compost 는 썩히다, 분해되다 로 그렇게 썩히거나 분해돼서 퇴비로 만들 수 있는 걸 말해요

+ Some people turn food waste into compost.

​

food waste processor

음식물 쓰레기 처리기

+ We got a food waste processor to deal with leftovers. It’s amazing.

​

orange peel

오렌지 껍질

watermelon rind

수박 껍질

+ 보통 얇은 과일 껍질은 peel 이나 skin 을 쓰고 수박이나 멜론처럼 두껍고 딱딱한 껍질은 rind 를 써요

​

**[housework vs. chores vs. house chores]**

**housework**

집안일 전반 (청소, 설거지, 세탁 등 포함). 불가산 명사이므로 a housework라고는 하지 않음.

+ I do most of the housework on weekends.

​

**chores**

자잘하고 반복적인 일. 반드시 집안일에만 국한되지 않음.

+ Doing the same chores every day is tiring.

​

**house chores**

"chores"를 집안일로 구체화한 표현.

+ I usually split house chores with my roommate.
```

items.title: 20250716 Money Management
items.snippet: **1) Personal Habits & Experience** - What strategies do you use to keep track of your monthly spending? - How do your spending habits change when you’re stress
items.body_raw:
**1) Personal Habits & Experience**
- What strategies do you use to keep track of your monthly spending?
- How do your spending habits change when you’re stressed or busy?
- What’s the most valuable lesson you’ve learned about money from your past experience?
- Tell me about a time when you saved money successfully. What helped you stay consistent?

**2) Opinions & Values**
- What does “financial freedom” mean to you, and how do you plan to reach it?
- How do you decide whether something is worth spending money on or not?
- What do you think is the biggest mistake people make with their money?
- In your opinion, what role does money play in a happy life?

**3) Hypothetical & Future Thinking**
- Imagine you earn double your current income—how would your lifestyle change?
- How would you help a teenager understand the importance of saving money?
- Describe your ideal monthly budget. What would you prioritize?
- What would you do if you suddenly had to live without a credit or debit card for a month?

credit score 신용 점수
A good **credit score** can help you get a loan with a lower interest rate.
좋은 신용 점수는 더 낮은 이자율로 대출을 받는 데 도움이 된다.

Every little bit adds up 티끌모아태산
Saving a little money every day may seem small, but **every little bit adds up** over time.
매일 조금씩 저축하는 건 작아 보여도 시간이 지나면 티끌 모아 태산이다.

drained 매우 지친, 기운이 빠진, 에너지가 다 빠진 상태 (원래 뜻은 물을 빼내다, 배수하다)
After the long meeting, she felt completely **drained**.
긴 회의 후에 그녀는 완전히 기운이 빠졌어.

out of order X 이건 재고소진 ,망가진걸 말할떄는 Broken 고장 난, 망가진, 부서진
I need to fix the **broken** screen on my phone.
내 핸드폰 깨진 화면을 고쳐야 해.

용돈 allowance
My parents give me a weekly **allowance** to teach me about money.

​돈이 돈을 굴린다, Money makes money
If you invest wisely, **money makes money** over time.
현명하게 투자하면 돈이 시간이 지나면서 돈을 번다.

items.title: 20250722 Accommodation
items.snippet: **1) Personal Experience** -What kind of accommodation do you usually choose when you travel? -Have you ever stayed in a guesthouse? How was it? -Have you ever
items.body_raw:
**1) Personal Experience**
-What kind of accommodation do you usually choose when you travel?
-Have you ever stayed in a guesthouse? How was it?
-Have you ever used Airbnb or a similar service?
-What’s the most expensive accommodation in your life?
-Have you ever complained to the front desk? What happened?

**2) Comfort and Essentials**
-What items do you always check for in a hotel room?
-What annoys you the most in a poorly maintained room?
-What small details make a room feel luxurious to you?
-Would you rather stay at a hotel with a great view or in a perfect location?

**3) Personal Preferences**
-Would you stay in a place without a TV or internet?
-What would your dream hotel room look like?
-If you had enough money, would you prefer staying alone in a hotel or sharing it with your friends?
-Would you try a homestay with a local family?

▪️facilities
: 시설
"It was helpful to have laundry facilities during our long trip."

▪️stay duration
: 숙박 기간
"What’s the maximum stay duration for this property?"

▪️nightly rate
: 1박 요금
"The nightly rate was higher on the weekend."

▪️double occupancy
: 2인 기준
"The price includes double occupancy, not per person."

▪️overbooked
: 초과 예약된
"The hotel was overbooked, so they moved us to a partner hotel."

▪️no vacancy
: 빈방 없음
"We drove past several motels, but they all had no vacancy signs."

▪️complimentary
: 무료 아침 식사
"A complimentary breakfast was included in the room rate."

▪️climate control
: 온도 조절 시스템
"The room had individual climate control, which was nice."

▪️guest rating
: 고객 평점
"The guest rating was 4.9 stars, so we felt confident booking."

▪️carry-on bag
: 기내 반입 가방
"She fit everything she needed into one carry-on bag."

items.title: 20250729 Goals

items.body_raw:
**1) Personal Goals**
- What personal goals are you working on right now?
- Have you ever set a goal and achieved it? What was it?
- Do you write down your personal goals? Why or why not?
- How do you stay motivated to reach your personal goals?
- What personal goal has changed your life the most?

**2) Career & Academic Goals**
- What career goals do you have for the next five years?
- How do you plan to reach your dream job?
- Have you ever changed your career goals? Why?
- What kind of academic goals did you have as a student?

**3) Lifestyle & Health Goals**
- How do you plan to maintain a healthy lifestyle?
- What’s one fitness goal you’ve achieved?
- Have you ever set a goal to break a bad habit?
- Have you ever reached a goal and then lost motivation?

▪️hard-earned
: 어렵게 얻은
"It was a hard-earned promotion after years of dedication."

▪️set a high standard
: 높은 기준을 세우다
"She set a high standard for future employees."

▪️career breakthrough
: 직업상의 돌파구
"That award was his career breakthrough."

▪️long-term achievement
: 장기적인 성취
"Writing a novel is a long-term achievement that takes patience."

▪️sense of fulfillment
: 충만감, 만족감
"Volunteering gives me a sense of fulfillment."

▪️push one’s limits
: 자신의 한계를 넘어서다
"He pushed his limits to climb the mountain."

▪️dream come true
: 꿈이 현실이 되다
"Studying abroad was a dream come true."

▪️earn a title
: 타이틀을 따다
"She earned the title of national champion."

▪️win an award
: 상을 받다
"The film won several international awards."

▪️be recognized for
: ~로 인정받다
"She was recognized for her leadership and hard work."

items.title: 20250731 Water Activities

items.body_raw:
**1) Personal Experience & Memories**
- When did you first learn how to swim?
- What’s your favorite water activity?
- How old were you when you first visited a water park? What was it like?
- Do you have a memorable summer vacation that involved water activities? What made it special?

**2) Facilities & Preferences**
- What makes a great water park or public pool?
- Do you prefer water parks or beaches? Why?
- Do you like using hot tubs, saunas, or steam rooms?
- If you could design your ideal water park, what would it include?
- What kind of amenities or services do you look for when choosing a swimming pool or spa?

**3) Safety & Cleanliness**
- Do you ever worry about hygiene or cleanliness at public swimming pools?
- What hygiene habits are important when visiting public pools?
- Have you or someone you know ever had an accident or close call in the water?

▪️go swimming
: 수영하러 가다
"We went swimming in the lake near our cabin."

▪️swim instructor
: 수영 강사
"Our swim instructor is very patient and encouraging."

▪️swimming lanes
: 수영 레인
"Stay in your swimming lane during practice."

▪️improve technique
: 기술을 향상시키다
"She’s working hard to improve her technique."

▪️backstroke
: 배영
"The backstroke was harder for me than freestyle."

▪️underwater swimming
: 수중 수영
"We practiced underwater swimming with our goggles on."

▪️float on a tube
: 튜브 위에 둥둥 떠 있다
"Sometimes I just like to float on a tube and relax."

▪️ride a banana boat
: 바나나보트 타다
"We rode a banana boat and almost fell off from laughing so much."

▪️surf the waves
: 파도를 타다
"I want to learn how to surf the waves in Hawaii."

▪️public bathhouse
: 대중 목욕탕
"Many neighborhoods still have a traditional public bathhouse."

items.title: 20250805 Highschool
items.snippet: **1) Daily Life at High School** **- Did you enjoy going to high school?** 주요 표현 enjoy - 즐기다 look forward to - 기대하다 routine - 일상 hang out with friends - 친구들과 어울
items.body_raw:
**1) Daily Life at High School**

**- Did you enjoy going to high school?**
주요 표현
enjoy - 즐기다
look forward to - 기대하다
routine - 일상
hang out with friends - 친구들과 어울리다
memorable - 기억에 남는

“I looked forward to school mostly because I could hang out with my friends.”

**- What was your favorite subject in high school?**
주요 표현
be into ~ - ~에 관심이 많다
passionate about ~ - ~에 열정을 가진
excel at ~ - ~을 잘하다
math / science / literature / art / P.E. - 주요 과목들

“I was really into history. The stories about ancient civilizations just fascinated me.”

**- What subject did you dislike the most?**
주요 표현
struggle with ~ - ~을 어려워하다
not my cup of tea - 내가 좋아하는 스타일이 아닌
boring / stressful / confusing - 지루한 / 스트레스 주는 / 헷갈리는​

**- Was there a cafeteria or a school store in your high school?**
**- What did you usually do during lunch break?**
주요 표현
school cafeteria / school store - 급식실 / 매점
lunch options / snacks / best seller - 점심 메뉴 / 간식 / 인기 간식
hangout spot - 모이는 장소​


**2) People & Relationships**

**- Who was your favorite teacher in high school? Why?**
주요 표현
inspiring / supportive / strict but fair - 영감을 주는 / 지지해주는 / 엄격하지만 공정한
believed in me - 나를 믿어주다
motivated me - 동기부여해줬다

“Mr. Kim was strict, but he genuinely cared. He pushed me to do better.”

**- Are you still close with your high school friends?**
주요 표현
stay in touch - 연락을 유지하다
grew apart / drifted away - 멀어지다
lifelong friendship - 평생 친구

**- Did you enjoy school events with your classmates?**
주요 표현
school festival / sports day / field trip / overnight trip - 축제 / 운동회 / 소풍 / 수학여행
bonding time - 유대감 생기는 시간
good vibes / unforgettable memories - 좋은 분위기 / 잊지 못할 추억

**- What is one funny memory from high school?**
주요 표현
embarrassing / hilarious / unexpected - 민망한 / 아주 웃긴 / 예상 못 한
inside joke / prank / group chaos - 내부 농담 / 장난 / 단체 난장판​


**3) Looking Back & Reflective**

**- If you could go back to high school, what would you do differently?**
주요 표현
regret / do-over / make the most of ~ - 후회 / 다시 할 기회 / 최대한 활용하다
take things seriously / be more open - 좀 더 진지하게 / 더 개방적으로
focus on relationships / try new things - 인간관계에 집중하다 / 새로운 것을 시도하다

**- What was the biggest stress in high school?**
주요 표현
college entrance exam / pressure from parents / competition - 입시 / 부모님의 기대 / 경쟁
peer pressure / identity crisis - 또래 압박 / 정체성 혼란
academic burden / time management - 공부 스트레스 / 시간 관리

“Definitely the college entrance exams. It felt like everything depended on one test.”

**- What values or habits did you learn in high school?**
주요 표현
discipline / time management / teamwork / leadership - 자기관리 / 시간관리 / 팀워크 / 리더십
resilience / independence / responsibility - 회복력 / 독립성 / 책임감

**- Besides college entrance exam subjects, what high school class do you think is essential?**
주요 표현
life skills / financial literacy / mental health education / communication class
(생활 기술 / 금융 교육 / 정신 건강 교육 / 소통 수업)
real-world application - 실생활에 필요한
should be mandatory - 필수로 지정되어야 한다

▪️homeroom teacher
: 담임 선생님
"Our homeroom teacher checks attendance every morning."

▪️class president
: 반장
"She was elected class president by her classmates."

▪️after-school club
: 방과후 동아리
"I joined the photography club as my after-school activity."

▪️college entrance exam
: 대학 입시 시험
"He’s studying hard for the college entrance exam."

▪️mock exam
: 모의고사
"We take mock exams to prepare for the real entrance test."

▪️student council
: 학생회
"The student council organized the school festival this year."

▪️strict dress code
: 엄격한 복장 규정
"The school has a strict dress code for uniforms and hairstyles."

▪️school rules violation
: 교칙 위반
"He was warned for repeated school rules violations."

▪️school assembly
: 전교생 모임
"We had a school assembly about phone rules and safety."

▪️cutting class
: 수업을 몰래 빠지다
"Cutting class is risky—teachers always find out."

items.title: 20250807 Delivery Food
items.snippet: **1) Personal Experience & Habits** **- How often do you order food delivery?** order delivery → 배달을 시키다 frequency → 빈도 regularly → 정기적으로 once in a while → 가끔 a
items.body_raw:
**1) Personal Experience & Habits**

**- How often do you order food delivery?**
order delivery → 배달을 시키다
frequency → 빈도
regularly → 정기적으로
once in a while → 가끔
almost every day → 거의 매일

**- What types of food do you usually order?**
fast food → 패스트푸드
Korean dishes → 한식
fusion → 퓨전 음식
healthy food → 건강식
comfort food → 위로가 되는 음식

**- Do you stick with one app or try different delivery services?**
stick with → ~을 고수하다
try out → 시험 삼아 사용하다
delivery app → 배달 앱
service fee → 서비스 수수료
user interface → 사용자 인터페이스

**- Do you ever have problems with food delivery?**
late delivery → 배달 지연
wrong order → 잘못된 주문
cold food → 식은 음식
missing item → 빠진 음식
customer service → 고객 서비스


**2) Convenience & Cost**

**- How do you decide between delivery, takeout, and eating out?**
takeout → 포장
dine out → 외식하다
mood → 기분
weather → 날씨
convenience → 편리함

**- Do you think food delivery is worth the extra cost?**
worth it → 그만한 가치가 있는
delivery charge → 배달 요금
tip → 팁
time-saving → 시간 절약
expensive → 비싼

_"Even though it's more expensive, I think food delivery is worth it when I'm too tired to cook."_

**- Do you think people rely too much on food delivery these days?**
rely on → ~에 의존하다
too convenient → 너무 편리한
daily habit → 일상 습관
health impact → 건강에 미치는 영향
addictive → 중독성 있는

**- Have you noticed any price differences between apps or restaurants?**
price difference → 가격 차이
markup → 가격 인상
discount → 할인
in-app promotion → 앱 내 프로모션
hidden fee → 숨겨진 요금


**3) Culture & Social Impact**

**- Is food delivery changing the way people cook and eat together?**
home-cooked meal → 집밥
family time → 가족 시간
eating habits → 식습관
solo dining → 혼밥
cook less → 덜 요리하다

_"Because of food delivery, some families may spend less time cooking and eating together."_

**- How do food delivery habits differ across countries or cultures?**
eating culture → 식문화
tipping → 팁 문화
local food → 지역 음식
delivery speed → 배달 속도
popular items → 인기 메뉴

**- Do you think delivery workers should be paid more or offered better protections?**
delivery worker → 배달 노동자
fair pay → 공정한 임금
working conditions → 근무 환경
safety gear → 안전 장비
employment rights → 고용 권리

_"Yes, delivery workers face many risks and should receive better treatment and protections."_

**- How has the food delivery industry changed since the pandemic?**
pandemic → 팬데믹
increase in demand → 수요 증가
contactless delivery → 비대면 배달
health concerns → 건강 우려
growth in platform → 플랫폼 성장

▪️order in
: 배달시켜 먹다
"We were too tired to cook, so we decided to order in."

▪️go-to delivery place
: 단골 배달집
"What's your go-to delivery place when you're craving something greasy?"

▪️late-night delivery
: 야식 배달
"Late-night delivery is dangerous—I end up eating at 2 a.m. way too often."

▪️delivery fee
: 배달비
"Some places charge a crazy delivery fee even if it's close by."

▪️minimum order amount
: 최소 주문 금액
"I had to add a drink to meet the minimum order amount."

▪️menu scroll
: 배달앱에서 뭘 먹을지 한참 고민하기
“I lost track of time during my late-night menu scroll — everything looked so good.”

▪️delivery splurge
: 배달 음식으로 돈 펑펑 쓰는 날
"I had a delivery splurge after my final exams."

▪️delivery guilt
: 배달음식 너무 자주 시켜서 생기는 죄책감
"This is my third delivery this week... I really need to cook."

▪️delivery fail
: 배달 대참사, 실패한 경험
"It was a total delivery fail — spilled soup and cold rice."

▪️leftovers tomorrow
: 배달음식 남아서 다음날 먹기
"The pizza was huge — I'm saving the rest for lunch tomorrow."

items.title: 20250812 Gift
items.snippet: **1) Personal Gift Experiences** **- What’s the best gift you’ve ever received?** treasured → 소중히 여기는 memorable → 기억에 남는 hold onto → 간직하다 cherish → 아끼다, 소중히 하다
items.body_raw:
**1) Personal Gift Experiences**

**- What’s the best gift you’ve ever received?**
treasured → 소중히 여기는
memorable → 기억에 남는
hold onto → 간직하다
cherish → 아끼다, 소중히 하다
a gift to remember → 오래 기억할 선물

_"The watch my parents gave me for graduation is something I truly cherish and will hold onto forever."_

**- Have you ever received a gift you didn’t like but pretended to enjoy?**
insincere → 진심이 아닌
awkward → 어색한
put on a smile → 억지로 웃다
play along with → 맞장구치다, 맞춰주다, 동의하는 척하다
grin and bear it → 억지로 참다

_"I didn’t like the sweater at all, but I just put on a smile and decided to grin and bear it."_

**- What’s the most expensive gift you’ve ever received?**
luxurious → 고급스러운
extravagant → 사치스러운
splash out on → 큰돈을 쓰다
show off → 자랑하다
cost an arm and a leg → 엄청 비싸다

_"The diamond necklace must have cost an arm and a leg, but my friend never tried to show off about it."_

**- Has a gift ever made you emotional or cry?**
heartfelt → 진심 어린
sentimental → 감정적인
tear up → 눈물이 나다
open up → 마음을 열다
hit me right in the feels → 마음을 울리다

_"When I read the letter that came with the gift, I couldn’t help but tear up—it really hit me right in the feels."_

**- Have you ever received the same gift more than once?**
identical → 똑같은
repetitive → 반복적인
end up with → 결국 ~을 가지게 되다
give away → 주다, 양도하다
déjà vu → 데자뷔 같은 느낌

_"I’ve ended up with three identical mugs over the years—it was like déjà vu every birthday."_


**2) Gift Preferences & Opinions**

**- Do you prefer practical gifts or sentimental gifts?**
functional → 실용적인
emotional → 감정적인
lean toward → ~쪽으로 기울다
depend on → ~에 따라 다르다
hit close to home → 마음에 와닿다

_"I usually lean toward functional gifts, but sometimes a sentimental one can really hit close to home."_

**- Would you rather receive one big gift or many small ones?**
substantial → 상당한, 큰
varied → 다양한
break down → 나누다
spread out → 분산하다
quality over quantity → 양보다 질

_"I prefer one substantial gift—always quality over quantity for me."_

**- Do you like gift cards as presents?**
versatile → 다용도의
convenient → 편리한
stock up on → 비축하다
use up → 다 쓰다
the gift of choice → 선택의 자유를 주는 선물

_"Gift cards give me the gift of choice, so I can stock up on what I really want."_

**- Do you think giving cash is too impersonal?**
thoughtless → 성의 없는
straightforward → 직설적인, 단순한
hand over → 건네주다
miss out on → 놓치다
money talks → 돈이 모든 것을 가능하게 한다

_"Some say giving cash is thoughtless, but honestly, sometimes money talks."_

**- Would you be happy if someone gave you food as a gift?**
delicious → 맛있는
homemade → 집에서 만든
dig into → 맛있게 먹다
wrap up → 포장하다
the way to someone’s heart is through their stomach → 음식이 최고의 선물

_"If it’s homemade, I’ll gladly dig into it—after all, the way to someone’s heart is through their stomach."_


**3) Creative & Unusual Gifts**

**- What’s the most unique gift you’ve ever seen?**
one-of-a-kind → 유일한
memorable → 기억에 남는
stand out → 눈에 띄다
come across → 우연히 발견하다
like nothing else → 전혀 다른, 특별한

_"The handmade wooden music box really stood out—it was like nothing else I’ve ever seen."_

**- Have you ever received a funny or joke gift?**
hilarious → 아주 웃긴
silly → 유치한
burst out laughing → 폭소하다
play a prank on → 장난치다
pull someone’s leg → 놀리다

_"My friend once gave me a giant rubber chicken just to pull my leg, and I burst out laughing."_

**- Have you ever made a surprise gift for someone without a reason?**
spontaneous → 즉흥적인
thoughtful → 사려 깊은
whip up → 급히 만들다
hand out → 건네주다
out of the blue → 불쑥, 느닷없이

_"I once whipped up cookies and gave them to my neighbor out of the blue—she was so happy."_

**- If you could give yourself a gift right now, what would it be?**
deserve → ~할 자격이 있다
indulgent → 사치스러운, 즐기는
treat myself to → 나 자신에게 ~를 선물하다
save up for → ~를 위해 저축하다
splurge on → 큰돈을 쓰다

_"I would treat myself to a luxury spa day and just splurge on relaxation."_

▪️wish list
: 받고 싶은 선물 목록
“I sent him my wish list so he wouldn’t have to guess.”

▪️surprise gift
: 깜짝 선물
“He left a surprise gift on my desk before I arrived at work.”

▪️gift card
: 상품권, 기프트 카드
“I bought her a coffee shop gift card since she goes there every morning.”

▪️gift bag
: 선물 봉투
“I forgot wrapping paper, so I used a gift bag instead.”

▪️anniversary gift
: 기념일 선물
“They exchanged small anniversary gifts over dinner.”

▪️last-minute gift
: 급하게 준비한 선물
“That was a last-minute gift, but she loved it anyway.”

▪️practical
: 실용적인
“A vacuum might not be exciting, but it’s a practical gift.”

▪️ceremonial
: 의례적인
"A ceremonial gift is exchanged during official events."

▪️gift-giving culture
: 선물 문화
"In some workplaces, the gift-giving culture encourages small tokens of appreciation during holidays."

▪️gift as a gesture
: 마음을 담은 선물
"A gift as a gesture can mean more than its actual price."

items.title: 20250814 Birthday
items.snippet: **1) Personal Experiences & Celebrations** **- How do you usually celebrate your birthday?** tradition → 전통 guest list → 초대 명단 set up → 준비하다, 설치하다 throw (a part
items.body_raw:
**1) Personal Experiences & Celebrations**

**- How do you usually celebrate your birthday?**
tradition → 전통
guest list → 초대 명단
set up → 준비하다, 설치하다
throw (a party) → (파티를) 열다
make a big deal out of it → 대대적으로 챙기다

_“I don’t usually make a big deal out of my birthday, but sometimes I throw a small dinner with friends.”_

**- Have you ever planned a birthday party for someone else? How did it go?**
venue → 장소
budget → 예산
put together → 준비하다
work out → 잘 되다, 성공하다
go well → 잘 진행되다

_“The surprise party I put together for my best friend went really well.”_

**- Do you like big parties or small gatherings for birthdays?**
crowd → 군중
atmosphere → 분위기
hang out → 어울리다
chill out → 편하게 쉬다
have a good time → 즐거운 시간을 보내다

_“I prefer small gatherings where I can just hang out and have a good time with close friends.”_


**2) Gifts & Giving**

**- How do you usually choose a birthday gift for someone?**
hobby → 취미
wishlist → 희망 목록
pick out → 고르다
look for → 찾다
just right → 딱 맞는

_“If you know their hobby, you can pick out something that feels just right for them.”_

**- Do you prefer giving or receiving gifts on birthdays? Why?**
gesture → 제스처, 표현
surprise → 놀라움
hand over → 건네주다
open up → (선물을) 열다
give and take → 주고받기

_“For me, birthdays are all about give and take, but I enjoy handing over a gift more than opening one.”_

**- Do you prefer practical gifts or surprise gifts?**
necessity → 필수품
novelty → 새로움
go for → 선택하다
wrap up → 포장하다
out of the ordinary → 평범하지 않은, 특별한

_“I usually go for practical gifts, but once in a while I like something out of the ordinary.”_


**3) Feelings & Reflections**

**- How do you feel about getting older and birthdays in general?**
milestone → 중요한 시점
mindset → 사고방식
look back on → 되돌아보다
look forward to → 기대하다
age is just a number → 나이는 숫자에 불과하다

_“I look back on my past birthdays and remind myself that age is just a number.”_

**- What’s one birthday memory that stands out the most to you?**
highlight → 하이라이트, 가장 좋은 순간
occasion → 행사, 경우
stick out → 눈에 띄다
look back at → 되돌아보다
never forget → 절대 잊지 않다

_“My 18th birthday trip is something I’ll never forget.”_

**- Do you think birthdays are more important for children or adults?**
priority → 우선순위
grow up → 자라다
care about → 신경 쓰다
make someone’s day → 누군가를 행복하게 해주다

_“Birthdays might make a child’s day more than an adult’s, but they matter for everyone.”_

▪️headcount
: 예상 인원 수
"What’s the headcount so I can order enough pizza?"

▪️venue
: 행사 장소
"We changed the venue to a rooftop bar with a great view."

▪️birthday honoree
: 생일 주인공
"All eyes were on the birthday honoree as she entered the room."

▪️birthday treat
: 생일 특별한 대접
"He’s taking us to a theme park as his birthday treat."

▪️intimate gathering
: 소규모 친밀한 모임
"They preferred an intimate gathering over a big birthday party."

▪️lavish celebration
: 호화로운 축하 행사
"They hosted a lavish celebration with over 200 guests."

▪️birthday bash
: 성대한 생일 파티
"We’re hosting a big birthday bash for her 30th."

▪️surprise reception
: 깜짝 환영회
"The surprise reception left the birthday guest speechless."

▪️shared birthday
: 같은 날 생일
"We found out we have a shared birthday!"

▪️belated birthday
: 늦은 생일 인사
"Sorry for the belated birthday text!"

items.title: 20250826 Team Work
items.snippet: **1) Teamwork & Collaboration** **- What makes a team successful?** work together → 함께 일하다 pull through → 어려움을 극복하다 cohesion → 결속 synergy → 시너지 on the same wave
items.body_raw:
**1) Teamwork & Collaboration**

**- What makes a team successful?**
work together → 함께 일하다
pull through → 어려움을 극복하다
cohesion → 결속
synergy → 시너지
on the same wavelength → 마음이 잘 맞는

_“A team becomes truly successful when its members are on the same wavelength and can pull through challenges with strong cohesion.”_

**- How do you usually contribute to a group project?**
step up → 앞장서다
pitch in → 돕다
initiative → 주도성
accountability → 책임감
pull your weight → 몫을 다하다

_“In group projects, I try to step up and pitch in wherever I can, because pulling my weight shows accountability.”_

**- What’s the hardest part of working in a team?**
deal with → 다루다
put up with → 참다
ego → 자존심
compromise → 타협
not on the same page → 생각이 안 맞는

_“The hardest part is dealing with big egos when people are not on the same page.”_

**- Do you prefer working alone or in a group?**
lean on → 의지하다
depend on → 의존하다
autonomy → 자율성
reliance → 의존
two heads are better than one (백지장도 맞들면 낫다)

_“Even though I enjoy autonomy, two heads are often better than one when solving tough problems.”_


**2) Motivation & Goals in Teams**

**- What motivates you the most in a team or group?**
look forward to → 기대하다
push through → 극복하다
recognition → 인정
collaboration → 협력
pulling in the same direction → 한 방향으로 힘을 합치는 것

_“What motivates me most is looking forward to collaboration where everyone pushes through obstacles, and recognition ensures we are all pulling in the same direction.”_

**- Do you think clear goals are always necessary for teams?**
lay out → 설명하다
stick to → 고수하다
direction → 방향
clarity → 명확성
crystal clear → 매우 명확한

_“Without crystal clear goals, teams can easily lose direction.”_

**- What keeps long-term projects moving forward?**
carry on → 계속하다
follow through → 끝까지 하다
commitment → 헌신
persistence → 끈기
keep the momentum → 추진력을 유지하다

_“Persistence and commitment help keep the momentum alive.”_

**- Have you ever had a personal breakthrough in a team?**
open up → 마음을 열다
level up → 향상되다
confidence → 자신감
discovery → 발견
lightbulb moment → 번뜩임의 순간

_“Working in a team gave me a lightbulb moment about my strengths.”_


**3) Team Experiences & Culture**

**- What’s the most memorable team you’ve ever been part of?**
look back on → 회상하다
stand out → 두드러지다
memories → 추억
bond → 유대
once-in-a-lifetime → 일생에 한 번뿐인

_“When I look back on my university debate team, it still stands out as a once-in-a-lifetime experience that shaped not only my confidence but also my lifelong friendships.”_

**- Have you ever been in a sports team?**
train for → 훈련하다
cheer on → 응원하다
discipline → 훈련
harmony → 조화
play your part → 역할을 다하다

_“Being in a soccer team taught me that no matter how skilled you are individually, the moment you fail to play your part, the entire strategy collapses.”_

**- How do you usually feel in large groups?**
blend in → 섞이다
stand out → 두드러지다
crowd → 군중
anxiety → 불안
lost in the crowd → 군중 속에서 사라지다

_“In large groups, I sometimes blend in too much and feel lost in the crowd, which makes it harder for my opinions to stand out.”_

**- Have you ever joined a volunteer team?**
give back → 돌려주다
sign up for → 참여하다
bond → 유대
impact → 영향
lend a helping hand → 도움을 주다

_“Joining a volunteer team gave me the chance to give back to society and lend a helping hand to people in real need.”_

▪️ collaboration
: 협력, 공동 작업
“Good collaboration makes the project successful.”
“좋은 협력이 프로젝트를 성공하게 만든다.”

▪️ division of roles
: 역할 분담
“The team had a clear division of roles.”
“팀은 명확한 역할 분담을 했다.”

▪️ team spirit
: 팀 정신
“Her team spirit encouraged everyone.”
“그녀의 팀 정신이 모두를 고무시켰다.”

▪️ shared responsibility
: 공동 책임
“Shared responsibility makes members more careful.”
“공동 책임은 팀원들을 더 신중하게 만든다.”

▪️ conflict resolution
: 갈등 해결
“Conflict resolution skills are essential in teamwork.”
“팀워크에서 갈등 해결 능력은 필수적이다.”

▪️ peer support
: 동료 지원
“Peer support helped him finish the tough task.”
“동료들의 지원 덕분에 그는 힘든 일을 끝낼 수 있었다.”

▪️ decision-making process
: 의사 결정 과정
“The decision-making process included every member.”
“의사 결정 과정에는 모든 팀원이 참여했다.”

▪️ consensus
: 합의, 의견 일치
“The team reached a consensus after discussion.”
“팀은 논의 끝에 합의에 도달했다.”

▪️ mutual respect
: 상호 존중
“Mutual respect makes teamwork smoother.”
“상호 존중은 팀워크를 원활하게 한다.”

▪️ accountability
: 책임 의식
“Each member has accountability for their task.”
“각 팀원은 자신의 일에 책임 의식을 가진다.”

items.title: 20250828 Sleep Habit
items.snippet: **1) Quality of Sleep** **- Do you usually sleep through the night or wake up often?** insomnia → 불면증 pattern → 패턴 sleep through → 잠에서 깨지 않고 자다 wake up → 깨다 tos
items.body_raw:
**1) Quality of Sleep**

**- Do you usually sleep through the night or wake up often?**
insomnia → 불면증
pattern → 패턴
sleep through → 잠에서 깨지 않고 자다
wake up → 깨다
toss and turn → (잠이 안 와서) 뒤척이다

_“On stressful days, I toss and turn so much that I barely sleep through the night.”_

**- What makes it hard for you to fall asleep?**
stress → 스트레스
habit → 습관
stay up → 늦게까지 깨어 있다
wind down → 긴장을 풀다
keep me up → 잠 못 들게 하다

_“Scrolling on my phone always keeps me up longer than I planned.”_

**- Do you feel more like a light sleeper or a heavy sleeper?**
rest → 휴식
cycle → 주기
drift off → 잠들다
wake up to → ~에 깨어나다
sleep like a rock → 돌처럼 깊이 자다

_“Unlike my sister who can sleep like a rock, I wake up to even the faintest sound.”_

**- How many hours of sleep do you usually get on weekdays vs. weekends?**
average → 평균
lack of sleep → 수면 부족
catch up on → (잠을) 만회하다
well-rested → 충분히 쉰
oversleep → 늦잠 자다

_“On weekdays I usually lack sleep, so I try to catch up on rest during weekends.”_


**2) Environment & Comfort**

**- Do you prefer sleeping in silence or with background noise (like music or white noise)?**
silence → 고요
noise → 소음
block out → 차단하다
fall asleep to → ~를 들으며 잠들다
peace and quiet → 평화롭고 조용한 상태

_“I usually fall asleep to soft music, but when I’m exhausted, I crave complete peace and quiet.”_

**- Do you like sleeping in total darkness or with some light?**
darkness → 어둠
comfort → 편안함
turn off → 끄다
leave on → 켜 두다
pitch black → 칠흑같이 어두운

_“I can only rest well when the room is pitch black, otherwise I wake up easily.”_

**- Do you sleep better in your own bed or when traveling?**
pillow → 베개
routine → 일상
settle in → 자리를 잡다
get used to → 익숙해지다
home sweet home → 집만 한 데는 없다

_“No matter how comfortable the hotel is, I sleep best at home—home sweet home.”_


**3) Fun & Personal**

**- What’s the strangest dream you remember?**
dream → 꿈
memory → 기억
wake up from → ~에서 깨어나다
think back on → 되돌아보다
feel so real → 너무 현실 같게 느껴지다

_“I still think back on a dream that felt so real I was confused when I woke up.”_

**- Do you talk, snore, or move in your sleep?**
snore → 코를 골다
movement → 움직임
talk in → (잠꼬대를) 하다
roll over → 몸을 굴리다
not even know it → 자신은 전혀 모른다

_“Apparently, I snore and roll over all night, but I don’t even know it.”_

**- Do you prefer mornings or late nights?**
morning person → 아침형 인간
night owl → 올빼미형 인간
get up → 일어나다
burn the midnight oil → 밤늦게까지 공부하거나 일하다

_“I’m more of a night owl, often burning the midnight oil while my friends are already asleep.”_

**- Have you ever fallen asleep in an unusual place (bus, class, office)?**
doze off → 깜빡 잠들다
nod off → 졸다
embarrassing → 당황스러운
exhausted → 매우 피곤한
catnap → 짧은 낮잠

_“Once I dozed off on the subway, and it was so embarrassing when I almost missed my stop.”_

▪️ early bird
: 아침형 인간
“She’s an early bird who enjoys jogging at dawn.”

▪️ night owl
: 올빼미형 인간 (늦게 자고 늦게 일어나는 사람)
“He’s a night owl who works best after midnight.”

▪️ insomnia
: 불면증
“Stress often leads to insomnia.”

▪️ broken sleep
: 끊어진 잠, 자주 깨는 수면
“His broken sleep made him feel tired in the morning.”

▪️ sleep paralysis
: 가위 눌림
“Some people find sleep paralysis frightening.”

▪️ digital distraction before bed
: 취침 전 디지털 방해 요소
“Digital distraction before bed makes it harder to fall asleep.”

▪️ oversleeping
: 과도한 수면, 늦잠
“He missed his meeting because of oversleeping.”

▪️ restorative sleep
: 회복성 수면
“Restorative sleep helps the body repair itself.”

▪️ sleep efficiency
: 수면 효율 (잠든 시간 대비 실제로 잔 시간의 비율)
“His doctor measured his sleep efficiency using a test.”

▪️ dream recall
: 꿈 기억
“Dream recall improves if you write down dreams immediately.”

---
### 💤 Paragraph 1 – Quality of Sleep

On stressful days, I **toss and turn** so much that I barely **sleep through** the night. Sometimes I even **wake up** several times without knowing why. I think my **sleep pattern** is quite irregular, and on weekdays, I usually suffer from **lack of sleep**. That’s why I try to **catch up on rest** during weekends.

---
### 🛏️ Paragraph 2 – Environment & Comfort

When it comes to comfort, I usually **fall asleep to** soft music because it helps me **wind down**. But if I’m really tired, I prefer complete **silence** and **peace and quiet**. I can only **rest** well when the room is **pitch black**, otherwise I **wake up to** even the faintest light. No matter how comfortable a hotel is, I always sleep best at home—**home sweet home**.

---
### 🌙 Paragraph 3 – Dreams & Sleep Habits

I still **think back on** a dream that **felt so real** I was confused when I **woke up from** it. Apparently, I **snore** and sometimes **roll over** a lot at night, but I **don’t even know it**. I guess that makes me more of a **light sleeper**. On the other hand, my sister can **sleep like a rock** without any **broken sleep** at all.

---
### ⏰ Paragraph 4 – Morning or Night

Honestly, I’m more of a **night owl**. I usually **stay up** late and often **burn the midnight oil**, whether I’m working or just distracted by my phone. That habit always **keeps me up** longer than I planned. I really envy **early birds** who **get up** at dawn feeling fresh and **well-rested**.

---
### 🚇 Paragraph 5 – Unusual Sleeping Places

Once, I completely **dozed off** on the subway because I was so **exhausted**. It was really **embarrassing** when I almost missed my stop. Sometimes, I also **nod off** in the office after lunch, but luckily it’s usually just a short **catnap**.

items.title: 20250902 Elementary School
items.snippet: **1) School Life** **- What was your favorite subject in elementary school?** look back on → 되돌아보다 focus on → 집중하다 assignment → 과제 memories → 추억 bring back memo
items.body_raw:
**1) School Life**

**- What was your favorite subject in elementary school?**
look back on → 되돌아보다
focus on → 집중하다
assignment → 과제
memories → 추억
bring back memories → 추억을 떠올리다

_“Looking back on my childhood, I realize how certain subjects shaped my way of thinking in ways I didn’t understand at the time.”_

**- Who was your favorite teacher in elementary school?**
look up to → 존경하다
get along with → 잘 지내다
discipline → 규율
consequence → 결과
make a difference → 변화를 일으키다

_“The teacher I looked up to had the rare ability to make even the most reluctant students feel motivated.”_

**- How did you usually spend your breaks between classes?**
run around → 이리저리 뛰다
cool down → 진정하다
recess → 쉬는 시간
playground → 운동장
blow off steam → 스트레스를 풀다

_“Recess gave children the chance to blow off steam, which was crucial for their focus in the next class.”_

**- How did you usually make new friends at school?**
reach out to → 다가가다
get to know → 알게 되다
popularity → 인기
bond → 유대
hit it off → 죽이 잘 맞다

_“It always amazed me how quickly children could hit it off with strangers and turn them into close friends within days.”_


**2) School Memories**

**- What is your most vivid memory from elementary school?**
bring back → 떠올리게 하다
stick with → 계속 남다
nostalgia → 향수
impression → 인상
etched in my mind, memory → 머릿속에 깊이 새겨진

_“Certain moments remain etched in my mind, reminding me how childhood impressions can last a lifetime.”_

**- Did you ever have a moment in elementary school that changed you?**
open up → 마음을 열다
grow up → 성숙해지다
turning point → 전환점
realization → 깨달음
a wake-up call (중요한 깨달음을 주는 사건)

_“One experience in elementary school became a wake-up call, forcing me to grow up faster than I expected.”_

**- Did you ever make a lifelong friend in elementary school?**
hang out with → 어울리다
stick by → 곁을 지키다
companionship → 우정, 동반자 관계
character → 성격, 인격
a friend for life → 평생 친구

_“Some childhood bonds are so deep that they turn into friendships for life, surviving distance and time.”_

**- Was there a moment when you felt especially proud in elementary school?**
pull off → 성공하다
look back on → 되돌아보다
achievement → 성취
recognition → 인정
on cloud nine → 너무 행복한

_“Pulling off a difficult task at a young age left me on cloud nine, and even now I look back on it with pride.”_


**3) Reflection & Growth**

**- What values did you learn in elementary school that still guide today?**
hold on to → 지키다
live up to → 부응하다
integrity → 정직
compassion → 연민
stand the test of time → 시간의 시험을 견디다

_“The sense of integrity and compassion instilled in me during those years has stood the test of time, influencing my choices to this day.”_

**- If you could relive one moment from elementary school, what would it be?**
do over → 다시 하다
deal with → 처리하다
regret → 후회
milestone → 중요한 사건
a trip down memory lane → 추억 여행

_“If I could relive any milestone, it would be a trip down memory lane to the day I first performed in front of the whole school.”_

**- How do you think elementary school friendships differ from adult friendships?**
drift apart → 서서히 멀어지다
stay in touch → 연락을 유지하다
authenticity → 진정성
obligation → 의무
grow apart → 점점 멀어지다

_“Elementary school friendships are often defined by authenticity, whereas adult relationships can sometimes be weighed down by obligations.”_

**- What kind of memories from elementary school do you want to pass down to your children?**
pass down → 전수하다
apply to → ~에 적용되다
heritage → 유산
innocence → 순수함
in hindsight → 돌이켜 보면

_“In hindsight, the innocence of those carefree days is the kind of heritage I would like my children to experience.”_

▪️team practice
: 팀 연습
“We have soccer team practice after school.”
방과 후에 축구팀 연습이 있습니다.

▪️school lunch
: 학교에서 제공하는 점심식사
“We had curry for school lunch today.”
오늘 급식으로 카레를 먹었습니다.

💡 Tip #1 - “lunch” 표현
“packed lunch" = 도시락
"lunch box" = 점심 도시락 통
"lunch break" = 점심시간

▪️tutoring
: 개인 과외
“I get math tutoring every Saturday.”
저는 매주 토요일 수학 과외를 받아요.

▪️line up
: 차례대로 줄을 서는 것
“Line up quietly before going to class.”
수업에 들어가기 전에 조용히 줄 서 주세요.

💡 Tip #2 - “quiet” vs. “silence”
“quiet" = 조용한 (Please be quiet)
“silence" = 완전한 침묵 (There was complete silence)

▪️field trip
: 학생들이 교실을 벗어나 견학이나 소풍을 가는 것
“We’re going on a field trip tomorrow.”
내일 현장 학습을 갑니다.

▪️private academy
: 사설 학원 (한국식 ‘학원’)
“Many students go to a private academy after school.”
많은 학생들이 방과 후에 학원에 다닙니다.

💡 Tip #3 - “academy” vs. “school”
“academy" = 특정 기술/과목을 가르치는 기관 (e.g. music academy)
"school" = 정규 교육 기관

items.title: 20250909 Study
items.snippet: **1) Study Habits & Routines** **- What kind of environment helps you study best?** cut off → 차단하다 focus on → 집중하다 ambiance → 분위기 concentration → 집중력 peace and
items.body_raw:
**1) Study Habits & Routines**

**- What kind of environment helps you study best?**
cut off → 차단하다
focus on → 집중하다
ambiance → 분위기
concentration → 집중력
peace and quiet → 평화롭고 조용한 환경

_“I find that peace and quiet help me focus on challenging material, and when I cut off distractions, even a small space can become a perfect study environment.”_

**- Do you prefer studying alone or in a group?**
bounce off → 서로 아이디어를 주고받다
split up → 나누다
collaboration → 협력
independence → 독립성
two heads are better than one → 두 명이 함께하면 더 낫다

_“Although I value independence, sometimes bouncing off ideas with others proves that two heads are better than one when tackling complex topics.”_

**- What is your biggest distraction while studying?**
zone out → 멍해지다
cut down on → 줄이다
temptation → 유혹
habit → 습관
lose track of time → 시간 가는 줄 모르다

_“Social media often tempts me to give in, and once I zone out scrolling, I completely lose track of time, so I try to cut down on usage.”_

**- What subject do you find most challenging to study?**
struggle with → 힘들어하다
catch up on → 따라잡다
complexity → 복잡성
comprehension → 이해
a steep learning curve → 배울 게 많아서 힘든 과정

_“Math presents a steep learning curve for me, so I often struggle with concepts but eventually figure them out by catching up on the basics.”_


**2) Study Strategies & Techniques**

**- What study techniques work best for you?**
build up → 쌓다
sort out → 정리하다
effectiveness → 효과
approach → 접근법
trial and error → 시행착오

_“Through trial and error, I realized that jotting down key words and sorting out main ideas helps me build up long-term memory more effectively.”_

**- How do you use technology to support your studies?**
look up → 찾아보다
take down → 받아 적다
resource → 자료
accessibility → 접근성
at your fingertips → 손끝에 바로 있는

_“With digital resources, I can log in anywhere, look up what I need instantly, and take down notes, making knowledge feel as if it’s always at my fingertips.”_

**- Do you prefer reading textbooks or watching videos to learn?**
skim through → 훑어보다
pause at → 멈추다
engagement → 몰입
preference → 선호
a picture is worth a thousand words → 그림 한 장이 천 마디 말보다 낫다

_“Although I often skim through textbooks for depth, I prefer videos because pausing at visuals shows how a picture is worth a thousand words in learning.”_

**- Do you find it helpful to teach others what you’ve learned?**
pass on → 전하다
explain away → 설명으로 풀다
reinforcement → 강화
clarity → 명확성
teach to learn → 가르쳐야 배운다

_“When I walk my peers through concepts and pass on explanations, I find that teaching to learn solidifies my clarity far better than silent review.”_


**3) Study Experiences & Reflections**

**- What’s the most memorable study experience you’ve ever had?**
look back on → 되돌아보다
stand out → 눈에 띄다
milestone → 이정표
recollection → 회상
once in a lifetime → 일생에 한 번 있는

_“When I look back on my school years, one study project stands out as a once-in-a-lifetime milestone that has always stuck with me."_

**- Have you ever studied abroad? What did you learn from it?**
set out → 떠나다
immerse in → 몰입하다
diversity → 다양성
adaptation → 적응
eye-opening experience → 시야를 넓히는 경험

_“When I set out to study abroad, I immersed myself in cultural diversity, and the biggest takeaway was an eye-opening experience of adaptation.”_

**- What study achievement are you most proud of?**
pay off → 보상받다
build up → 쌓다
accomplishment → 성취
recognition → 인정
hard work pays off → 노력은 보상받는다

_“When I look back on my achievements, the milestone I’m proudest of reminds me that hard work always pays off in the end.”_

**- What advice would you give to your younger self about studying?**
point out → 지적하다
build on → 발전시키다
regret → 후회
encouragement → 격려
better late than never → 늦더라도 안 하는 것보단 낫다

_“If I could look back and point out one thing, I’d tell my younger self to build on small steps earlier—better late than never.”_

▪️pull an all-nighter
: 밤새 공부하다
“She pulled an all-nighter to finish her project.”
그녀는 프로젝트를 끝내려고 밤을 새웠습니다.

▪️hit the books
: 열심히 공부하다 (책을 치다 → 공부 시작하다)
“I need to hit the books for my exam tomorrow.”
저는 내일 시험을 위해 열심히 공부해야 합니다.

💡 Tip #1 - “study” vs. “learn”
"study" = 배우기 위해 노력하다 (I study English every day)
"learn" = 실제로 습득하다 (I learned a new word today)

▪️stay focused
: 집중을 유지하다
“Try to stay focused during the lecture.”
강의 동안 집중을 유지하세요.

▪️zone out
: 멍하다, 집중이 끊기다
“I zoned out in the middle of the lesson.”
저는 수업 도중에 멍해졌습니다.

💡 Tip #2 - “late at night” vs. “all night”
"late at night" = 밤늦게 (but still sleep later)
"all night" = 밤새도록 (no sleep)

▪️hit a mental block
: 생각이 막히다
“I hit a mental block while solving the math problem.”
저는 수학 문제를 풀다가 머리가 막혔습니다.

▪️get in the zone
: 몰입하다, 집중 상태에 들어가다
“Once she’s in the zone, she studies for hours.”
그녀는 집중 상태에 들어가면 몇 시간이고 공부합니다.

💡 Tip #3 – “focus” + 전치사
"focus on ~" = ~에 집중하다 (Focus on your work.)
"be focused on ~" = ~에 집중되어 있다 (I’m focused on math.)

items.title: 20250925 Morality
items.snippet: 1️⃣ **Personal Beliefs & Choices** **- Do you think people are naturally good or bad?** nature → 본성 tendency → 성향 come down to → 결국 ~이 되다 grapple with → 씨름하다 a
items.body_raw:
1️⃣ **Personal Beliefs & Choices**

**- Do you think people are naturally good or bad?**
nature → 본성
tendency → 성향
come down to → 결국 ~이 되다
grapple with → 씨름하다
a gray area → 애매한 영역

_" When I grapple with the question of whether people are naturally good or bad, I often feel it’s a gray area that comes down to context and upbringing."_

**- Have you ever had to make a difficult moral choice?**
conscience → 양심
dilemma → 딜레마
weigh up → 저울질하다
go through with → 끝까지 하다
torn between two sides → 양쪽 사이에서 갈등하다

_"I was once torn between two sides when I had to weigh up loyalty to a friend against telling the truth in a difficult situation."_

**- Do you believe lying can ever be “good”?**
deception → 속임수
intention → 의도
cover up → 숨기다
justify → 정당화하다
for the greater good → 더 큰 선을 위해

_"Sometimes small deceptions can be justified for the greater good, like covering up a detail to protect someone’s feelings."_


2️⃣ **White Lies & Honesty**

**- Do you think it’s okay to lie about liking a gift?**
gratitude → 감사
gesture → 행위, 표시
make up → 지어내다
hold back → 숨기다
sugarcoat the truth → 진실을 좋게 포장하다

_"When someone gives me a gift, I usually sugarcoat the truth because I value the gesture more than my personal preference."_

**- Is “I’m on my way” still a lie if you haven’t left the house yet?**
excuse → 변명
intention → 의도
stretch the truth → 진실을 과장하다
put off → 미루다
bend the truth → 진실을 약간 왜곡하다

_"Saying ‘I’m on my way’ before leaving is just bending the truth a little, since the intention is usually to put off conflict rather than deceive."_

**- Would you tell a friend the truth if their haircut looked bad?**
appearance → 외모
opinion → 의견
hold back → 숨기다
speak up → 솔직히 말하다
honesty is the best policy → 정직이 최선이다

_"Even though honesty is the best policy, I sometimes hold back when speaking up could unnecessarily hurt my friend’s confidence."_


3️⃣ **Modern Dilemmas**

**- Is it wrong to use ChatGPT or AI to do homework?**
plagiarism → 표절
shortcut → 지름길
rely on → 의존하다
hand in → 제출하다
cut corners → 편법을 쓰다

_"Some students cut corners by relying too heavily on AI to hand in assignments, but whether it’s truly wrong depends on how much they learn from the process."_

**- Do you think using ad-blockers is unfair to creators?**
revenue → 수익
support → 지원하다
cut off → 끊다
rely on → 의존하다
a double-edged sword → 양날의 검

_"Ad-blockers feel like a double-edged sword, because while they cut off creators’ revenue, they also protect users from overwhelming ads."_

**- Do you think online behavior should follow the same moral rules as real life?**
ethics → 윤리
standard → 기준
live up to → 따르다
carry over → 이어지다
what you see is what you get → 있는 그대로

_"I believe moral standards should carry over online, because what you see is what you get isn’t always true in the digital world."_

▪️moral judgment
: 도덕적 판단
“His moral judgment guided the team through the crisis.”
그의 도덕적 판단이 팀을 위기에서 이끌었습니다.

▪️ethical behavior
: 윤리적 행동
“Ethical behavior is essential in medicine and law.”
윤리적 행동은 의학과 법률 분야에서 필수적입니다.

💡 Tip #1 – “virtue” vs. “quality” vs. “trait”
"virtue" = 도덕적 미덕 (patience is a virtue)
"quality" = 일반적인 성질/특성 (leadership quality)
"trait" = 개인적 특성 (honest trait)

▪️ethical violation
: 윤리 위반
“The company was fined for ethical violations.”
그 회사는 윤리 위반으로 벌금을 부과받았습니다.

▪️conflict of interest
: 이해 충돌
“Politicians should avoid any conflict of interest.”
정치인들은 어떠한 이해 충돌도 피해야 합니다.

💡 Tip #2 – “violation” vs. “breach”
"violation" = 규칙, 법, 윤리 위반 (ethical violation, traffic violation)
"breach" = 계약, 약속 위반 (breach of contract, breach of trust)

▪️honorable action
: 명예로운 행동
“Returning the lost wallet was an honorable action.”
잃어버린 지갑을 돌려준 것은 명예로운 행동이었습니다.

▪️moral courage
: 도덕적 용기
“Whistleblowers need moral courage to speak the truth.”
내부 고발자는 진실을 말할 도덕적 용기가 필요합니다.

💡 Tip #3 – “courage” vs. “bravery”
"courage" = 두려움 속에서도 옳은 행동 (moral courage)
"bravery" = 위험에 맞서는 용기 (physical bravery)

items.title: 20250930 Family
items.snippet: 1️⃣ **Family Background & Childhood** **-Did you resemble your mom or dad more when you were a child?** take after → 닮다 pass down → 물려주다 genes → 유전자 trait → 특징
items.body_raw:
1️⃣ **Family Background & Childhood**

**-Did you resemble your mom or dad more when you were a child?**
take after → 닮다
pass down → 물려주다
genes → 유전자
trait → 특징
chip off the old block → 부모와 판박이

_“Many people said I was a chip off the old block, taking after my father’s traits and appearance.”_

**-How many siblings do you have, and what was your relationship like?**
get along with → 잘 지내다
fall out with → 다투다
rivalry → 경쟁
closeness → 친밀함
blood is thicker than water→ 피는 물보다 진하다

_“Even though my siblings and I sometimes fell out, we always patched things up because blood is thicker than water.”_

**-What values did your family emphasize the most?**
stick to → 고수하다
lay down → 정하다
honesty → 정직
perseverance → 인내심
stand one’s ground→ 자기 입장을 고수하다

_“My parents laid down strict rules, which taught me to stand my ground in difficult situations.”_

**-Did your parents have different parenting styles?**
clash with → 충돌하다
go against → 거스르다
strictness → 엄격함
leniency → 관대함
good cop, bad cop → 한쪽은 엄격, 한쪽은 관대

_“Their parenting often seemed like a good cop, bad cop approach, but eventually they struck a balance.”_


2️⃣ **Daily Family Life**

**-Did your family often eat together?**
sit down to → ~을 함께 시작하다
finish off → 다 먹다
mealtime → 식사 시간
gathering → 모임
break bread together → 함께 식사하다

“Every mealtime was an opportunity to break bread together and strengthen our family _bond.”_

**-How did your family spend weekends?**
hang out → 어울리다
kick back → 쉬다
leisure → 여가
outing → 외출
quality time → 의미 있는 시간

_“Spending weekends outdoors allowed us to hang out and enjoy quality time together.”_

**-Who usually cooked at home, and what was their signature dish?**
whip up → 뚝딱 만들다
try out → 시험해 보다
ingredient → 재료
flavor → 맛
secret ingredient → 비밀 재료

_“My mother often whipped up meals with a secret ingredient, which made her dishes unforgettable.”_

**-How did your family celebrate birthdays?**
look forward to → 기대하다
set up → 준비하다
celebration → 축하
gathering → 모임
the life of the party→ 파티의 중심 인물

_“My sister always looked forward to birthdays because she loved being the life of the party.”_

​
3️⃣ **Extended Family & Relatives**

**-How often do you visit your relatives?**
drop by → 잠깐 들르다
keep in touch → 연락을 유지하다
relative → 친척
frequency → 빈도
long time no see → 오랜만이야

_“Whenever I dropped by my relatives’ house, it always felt like a long time no see moment.”_

**-What role did uncles or aunts play in your life?**
look up to → 존경하다
rely on → 의지하다
guidance → 지도
affection → 애정
like a second parent→ 제2의 부모 같은

_“I always looked up to my uncle because he acted like a second parent in my life.”_

**-Did you grow up close to your cousins?**
hang around with → 어울리다
fall out with → 사이가 틀어지다
companionship → 동료애
closeness → 친밀함
partners in crime → 장난꾸러기 동지

_“My cousins and I were like partners in crime, always hanging around together during childhood.”_

**-Did you have a favorite uncle, aunt, or cousin?**
look after → 돌보다
stick with → 함께하다
affection → 애정
bond → 유대감
near and dear to my heart → 내 마음에 가까운, 소중한

_“One cousin was especially near and dear to my heart because she always cheered me up.”_

▪️family tree
: 가계도, 족보
“He made a family tree for his history project.”
그는 역사 과제를 위해 가계도를 만들었습니다.

▪️distant relative
: 먼 친척
“I met a distant relative at the wedding.”
저는 결혼식에서 먼 친척을 만났습니다.

💡 Tip #1 – "relative" vs. "relation"
"relative" = 친척 (사람)
"relation" = 관계 (개념)
예) She is my relative.
예) I have a good relation with my uncle.

▪️in-laws
: 배우자의 가족 (시댁, 처가)
“My in-laws are visiting this weekend.”
제 시댁/처가 식구들이 이번 주말에 방문합니다.

▪️family bond
: 가족 간의 유대
“Strong family bonds help children feel secure.”
강한 가족 유대는 아이들이 안정감을 느끼도록 돕습니다.

💡 Tip #2 – "chore" vs. "housework"
"chore" = 하기 싫은 집안일 (빨래, 청소 등)
"housework" = 집안일 일반적 표현

▪️breadwinner
: 가장 (경제적으로 가족을 책임지는 사람)
“She became the family’s breadwinner after graduation.”
그녀는 졸업 후 가족의 가장이 되었습니다.

▪️household chores
: 집안일
“Everyone in the family helps with household chores.”
가족 모두가 집안일을 돕습니다.

💡 Tip #3 – "household" vs. "home"
"household" = 한 집에 사는 사람들 (경제·관리적 의미)
"home" = 집, 가정 (감정적·일상적 의미)

items.title: 20251014 Bucket List
items.snippet: 1️⃣ **Travel & Adventure Goals** **-Which country do you most want to visit before you die?** head to → ~로 향하다 set off → 출발하다 wanderlust → 여행욕구 curiosity → 호기심
items.body_raw:
1️⃣ **Travel & Adventure Goals**

**-Which country do you most want to visit before you die?**
head to → ~로 향하다
set off → 출발하다
wanderlust → 여행욕구
curiosity → 호기심
on my bucket list → 꼭 해보고 싶은 목록에 있는

_“Visiting Iceland has always been on my bucket list because of its surreal landscapes.”_

**-What’s the most adventurous thing you’ve ever done?**
take on → 도전하다
get through → 해내다
thrill → 스릴
courage → 용기
push one’s limits → 한계를 뛰어넘다

_“Skydiving helped me push my limits and realize how strong fear can make us.”_

**-Do you prefer solo travel or traveling with others?**
hang out with → 함께 어울리다
set out → 떠나다
companionship → 동행
solitude → 고독
fly solo → 혼자 행동하다

_“Traveling alone taught me to fly solo and discover who I truly am.”_

**-Would you rather explore nature or big cities?**
look around → 둘러보다
get away from → 벗어나다
skyscraper → 고층 건물
atmosphere → 분위기
off the beaten path → 잘 알려지지 않은 길

_“I prefer to travel off the beaten path and find hidden gems away from crowds.”_


2️⃣ **Career & Skill Goals**

**-What’s your ultimate career goal?**
work toward → 목표를 향해 노력하다
move up → 승진하다
promotion → 승진
dedication → 헌신
climb the corporate ladder → 회사에서 승진의 사다리를 오르다

_“I’m working toward a leadership role because I’ve always wanted to climb the corporate ladder.”_

**-What kind of skills do you want to master in your lifetime?**
work on → 향상시키다
build up → 쌓다
proficiency → 숙련도
self-improvement → 자기계발
jack of all trades→ 다방면에 능숙한 사람

_“I want to be a jack of all trades—able to adapt and learn new skills whenever needed.”_

**-Is starting your own business on your bucket list?**
start up → 창업하다
deal with → 처리하다
innovation → 혁신
strategy → 전략
take the plunge → 과감히 시작하다

_“One day, I’d love to take the plunge and start up my own company built on passion.”_

**-What kind of mentor or role model inspires you the most?**
look up to → 존경하다
count on → 의지하다
inspiration → 영감
wisdom → 지혜
a guiding light→ 인생의 길잡이

_“My mentor has always been a guiding light who helps me see my potential clearly.”_


3️⃣ **Relationship & Personal Connection Goals**

**-What kind of person do you hope to spend your life with?**
settle down with → 함께 정착하다
care for → 돌보다
companionship → 동반자 관계
affection → 애정
a match made in heaven → 천생연분

_“I hope to settle down with someone who feels like a match made in heaven.”_

**-Do you have any friendship goals on your bucket list?**
reach out to → 연락하다
open up to → 마음을 열다
connection → 유대감
sincerity → 진정성
through thick and thin → 좋을 때나 나쁠 때나 함께

_“True friends are those who stay with you through thick and thin.”_

**-Who has had the greatest emotional impact on your life?**
rely on → 의지하다
bring out → 이끌어내다
gratitude → 감사
empathy → 공감
leave a mark on someone → 누군가에게 깊은 인상을 남기다

_“My mother left a mark on me by showing what true empathy and strength look like.”_

**-What kind of person do you want to become for others?**
stand by → 지지하다
lift up → 격려하다
kindness → 친절
integrity → 진정성
be someone’s rock → 누군가의 든든한 버팀목이 되다

_“I want to be someone’s rock—steady, dependable, and full of warmth.”_

**On the process**
진행 중인, 과정에 있는
The project is still on the process, so we can’t share the results yet.

**Apply for**
~에 지원하다
She decided to apply for a scholarship to study abroad.

**Aurora**
오로라, 극광
We traveled to Norway to see the northern lights, the aurora.

**Backpacking**
배낭여행
He spent two months backpacking across Europe.

**Mesmerizing**
최면에 걸린 듯한, 매혹적인
The sunset over the ocean was absolutely mesmerizing.

**Nomad**
유목민; 떠돌이 생활을 하는 사람
Digital nomads can work from anywhere with a laptop and Wi-Fi.

**Culinary**
요리의, 음식과 관련된
She took a culinary class to improve her cooking skills.

**Companionship**
동반, 친구나 동료와의 교감
Traveling alone can be lonely, so I enjoy the companionship of friends.

**Get away from**
~에서 벗어나다, 떠나다
I need a vacation to get away from the busy city life.

**Skyscraper**
초고층 빌딩
The city skyline is full of impressive skyscrapers.

items.title: 20251016 Dealing with Illness
items.path: English - Langs Studio/20251016 Dealing with Illness.md
items.snippet: 1️⃣ **Personal Experiences** **- How do you usually deal with being sick — rest, medicine, or natural remedies?** symptom → 증상 remedy → 치료법 come down with → ~에
items.body_raw:
1️⃣ **Personal Experiences**

**- How do you usually deal with being sick — rest, medicine, or natural remedies?**
symptom → 증상
remedy → 치료법
come down with → ~에 걸리다
fight off → 이겨내다
under the weather → 몸이 안 좋은

_“When I’m feeling under the weather, I usually rest and drink tea instead of taking heavy medicine.”_

**- Do you go to the doctor easily, or only when it feels serious?**
appointment → 진료 예약
diagnosis → 진단
check up on → ~을 검사하다
put off → 미루다
better safe than sorry → 조심해서 나쁠 건 없다

_“I try not to put off doctor visits — it’s better safe than sorry when it comes to health.”_

**- What’s the most memorable time you were sick?**
fever → 열
recovery → 회복
get over → 회복하다
throw up → 토하다
knock me out → 기진맥진하게 하다

_“That stomach flu really knocked me out — I couldn’t eat anything for three days.”_


2️⃣ **Habits & Prevention**

**- Do you take vitamins or supplements regularly?**
supplement → 보충제
immune system → 면역 체계
stock up on → ~을 사두다
stick to → 꾸준히 하다
an apple a day keeps the doctor away → 사과 하나면 의사가 필요 없다

_“I try to stick to my vitamin routine — after all, an apple a day keeps the doctor away.”_

**- How do you try to stay healthy and avoid getting sick?**
hygiene → 위생
routine → 일상 습관
work out → 운동하다
cut down on → ~을 줄이다
prevention is better than cure → 예방이 치료보다 낫다

_“I work out regularly and cut down on junk food because prevention is better than cure.”_

**- Do you believe in traditional remedies or prefer modern medicine?**
herbal medicine → 한약, 약초 치료
prescription → 처방전
rely on → ~에 의지하다
turn to → ~에 기대다
old but gold → 옛것이지만 여전히 좋은

_“I sometimes turn to herbal medicine when I catch a cold — those old but gold remedies still work.”_


3️⃣ **Social & Cultural Perspectives**

**- Do you think it’s okay to go to work or school with a mild cold?**
contagious → 전염성의
sick leave → 병가
call in sick → 아프다고 연락하다
spread around → 퍼뜨리다
better to be safe than sorry → 조심해서 나쁠 건 없다

_“Even with a mild cold, I call in sick — it’s better to be safe than sorry and not spread it around.”_

**- Do you think people fake sickness sometimes to rest or escape responsibilities?**
excuse → 핑계
burnout → 탈진
make up → 지어내다
get away with → 교묘히 피하다
pull a sickie → 꾀병 부리다

_“Some people pull a sickie once in a while, especially when they’re burnt out from work.”_

**- How has COVID-19 changed how people think about illness?**
pandemic → 전염병 대유행
awareness → 인식
open up to → ~에 마음을 열다
adapt to → ~에 적응하다
hit close to home → 실감나게 다가오다

_“The pandemic really hit close to home — it made everyone more aware of hygiene and self-care.”_

▪️ home remedy
: 민간요법, 가정 요법
“My grandma’s home remedy for colds is honey tea.”
우리 할머니의 감기 민간요법은 꿀차예요.

▪️ over-the-counter medicine
: 일반의약품 (처방전 없이 살 수 있는 약)
“You can buy this cream as an over-the-counter medicine.”
이 크림은 일반의약품으로 살 수 있습니다.

💡 Tip #1 – ‘remedy’는 약뿐 아니라 ‘해결책’ 의미로도 자주 사용됨!
예: There’s no quick remedy for stress. (스트레스에는 빠른 해결책이 없다.)

▪️ catch up on rest
: 충분히 쉬다, 휴식을 보충하다
“After being sick, I just need to catch up on rest.”
아프고 나서 휴식을 좀 보충해야 해요.

▪️ recover from (an illness)
: 병에서 회복하다
“It took me a week to recover from the flu.”
독감에서 회복하는 데 일주일 걸렸어요.

💡 Tip #2 – ‘heal’ vs. ‘recover’
'heal' → 상처나 부상이 낫다 (My cut is healing fast.)
'recover' → 병이나 피로에서 회복하다 (She’s recovering from surgery.)

▪️ take sick leave
: 병가를 내다
“He had to take sick leave for a week.”
그는 일주일 동안 병가를 내야 했습니다.

▪️ feel under the weather
: 몸이 좀 안 좋은, 컨디션이 나쁜 (비격식 표현)
“I’m feeling a bit under the weather today.”
오늘은 몸이 좀 안 좋네요.

💡 Tip #3 – 일상회화에서 자주 쓰이는 표현!
“under the weather”는 “감기 걸린 것 같다”처럼 가벼운 몸살 상태를 의미합니다.

items.title: 20251021 Restaurant
items.snippet: 1️⃣ **Eating Out Habits** **- How often do you eat out?** go out for → ~하러 나가다 cut down on → 줄이다 frequency → 빈도 moderation → 절제 every now and then → 가끔씩 _“I try
items.body_raw:
1️⃣ **Eating Out Habits**

**- How often do you eat out?**
go out for → ~하러 나가다
cut down on → 줄이다
frequency → 빈도
moderation → 절제
every now and then → 가끔씩

_“I try to eat out only every now and then to stay healthy and save money.”_

**- What kind of restaurants do you usually go to?**
go for → 선택하다
try out → 시도하다
variety → 다양성
atmosphere → 분위기
hidden gem → 숨겨진 명소

_“I love checking out hidden gems that serve authentic local cuisine.”_

**- Do you care about a restaurant’s interior or just the food?**
pay attention to → 신경 쓰다
focus on → 집중하다
decor → 인테리어
impression → 인상
first impressions matter → 첫인상이 중요하다

_“I believe first impressions matter, so I tend to prefer places with warm ambiance.”_

**- How do you usually find new restaurants?**
come across → 우연히 발견하다
ask around → 물어보다
recommendation → 추천
platform → 플랫폼
word gets around → 소문이 퍼지다

_“Once a restaurant serves great food, word gets around quickly.”_


2️⃣ **Ordering & Dining Experience**

**- Do you prefer ordering the same food or trying something new?**
stick to → 고수하다
branch out → 새로운 걸 시도하다
curiosity → 호기심
comfort zone → 익숙한 영역
step out of your comfort zone → 익숙한 틀을 벗어나다

_“I try to step out of my comfort zone by ordering dishes I can’t even pronounce.”_

**- Have you ever complained about food at a restaurant?**
send back → 다시 돌려보내다
make up for → 보상하다
dissatisfaction → 불만
refund → 환불
the customer is always right → 손님이 항상 옳다

_“I rarely complain, but when the food is undercooked, I believe the customer is always right.”_

**- What do you usually do if a restaurant makes a mistake with your order?**
point out → 지적하다
make up for → 보상하다
misunderstanding → 오해
politeness → 공손함
handle it gracefully → 우아하게 대처하다

_“If there’s a mix-up, I try to handle it gracefully rather than making a scene.”_

**- Have you ever had a memorable dining experience?**
take in → 경험하다
end up → 결국 ~하게 되다
occasion → 특별한 순간
impression → 인상
once in a lifetime experience → 평생 한 번뿐인 경험

_“Dining on a rooftop in Paris was truly a once-in-a-lifetime experience.”_


3️⃣ **Memorable Food Experiences**

**- Have you ever regretted trying a particular dish?**
throw up → 토하다
swear off → 다시는 하지 않기로 하다
regret → 후회
discomfort → 불쾌함
live and learn→ 경험을 통해 배우다

_“I once tried blue cheese for the first time—let’s just say I lived and learned.”_

**- What’s the most expensive meal you’ve ever paid for?**
pay for → 지불하다
splurge on → 과하게 쓰다
luxury → 사치
experience → 경험
worth every penny → 한 푼도 아깝지 않은

_“That Michelin dinner was worth every penny—it was pure culinary art.”_

**- Have you ever had a meal that changed your opinion about food?**
open up to → 마음을 열다
learn from → 배우다
perception → 인식
transformation → 변화
eye-opening → 새로운 시각을 열어주는

_“Trying vegan cuisine for the first time was an eye-opening experience that changed my view on food.”_

**Work out**
운동하다
I try to work out three times a week to stay healthy.

**Eat out**
외식하다
We decided to eat out since no one wanted to cook tonight.

**Cardios**
유산소 운동
Doing cardios regularly helps improve heart health.

**Room temperature**
실온
Store the medicine at room temperature.

**Street food**
길거리 음식
Street food is often cheap and full of local flavor.

**Polarizing**
논란을 일으키는, 의견이 갈리는
The movie received polarizing reviews from critics.

**Fancy**
고급의, 화려한; ~하고 싶다
They went to a fancy restaurant for their anniversary dinner.

**Affordable**
가격이 알맞은, 부담 없는
The brand’s goal is to offer stylish yet affordable clothing.

**Play it safe**
안전하게 행동하다, 위험을 피하다
He decided to play it safe and invest in low-risk stocks.

**Search engine**
검색 엔진
Google is the most widely used search engine in the world.

**Ratings**
평가, 시청률
The show’s ratings increased after the new season premiered.

**Cafeteria**
구내식당, 카페테리아
The company cafeteria serves both Korean and Western food.

**Picky eater**
편식하는 사람
My little brother is such a picky eater — he only eats noodles.

**Can’t be bothered**
귀찮아서 ~하지 않다
I can’t be bothered to cook tonight, let’s just order delivery.

**Takes too long**
너무 오래 걸리다
I stopped going there because the service takes too long.

**Conglomerate**
대기업, 복합기업
Samsung is one of the largest conglomerates in South Korea.

**Business card**
명함
He handed me his business card after the meeting.

**Accommodation**
숙소, 숙박시설
We found affordable accommodation near the beach.

items.title: 20251028 Job Interview
items.snippet: 1️⃣ **Personal Background** **- Can you tell me about yourself?** sum up → 요약하다 point out → 강조하다 upbringing → 성장 과정 personality → 성격 in a nutshell → 간단히 말하자면 _“
items.body_raw:
1️⃣ **Personal Background**

**- Can you tell me about yourself?**
sum up → 요약하다
point out → 강조하다
upbringing → 성장 과정
personality → 성격
in a nutshell → 간단히 말하자면

_“In a nutshell, I’m a driven and detail-oriented person who thrives in fast-paced environments.”_

**- How would you describe yourself in three words?**
come up with → 생각해내다
stand out → 돋보이다
trait → 특성
reliability → 신뢰성
wear many hats → 여러 역할을 해내다

_“I’d describe myself as reliable, curious, and adaptable—I can wear many hats when needed.”_

**- What are your strengths?**
rely on → 의존하다
bring out → 이끌어내다
persistence → 끈기
initiative → 주도성
play to my strengths → 강점을 살리다

_“I always try to play to my strengths by staying organized and proactive in any project.”_

**- What are your weaknesses?**
work on → 개선하다
hold back → 억제하다
impatience → 조급함
tendency → 경향
a work in progress → 발전 중인 것

_“My perfectionism is still a work in progress—I’m learning to focus on impact, not just details.”_


2️⃣ **Skills & Experience**

**- Can you describe a project you’re proud of?**
carry out → 수행하다
pay off → 결실을 맺다
collaboration → 협력
outcome → 결과
take pride in → 자부심을 느끼다

_“I take pride in leading a cross-department project that increased efficiency by 30%.”_

**- How do you handle challenges or difficulties at work?**
cope with → 대처하다
push through → 끝까지 해내다
composure → 침착함
resourcefulness → 임기응변 능력
keep a cool head → 냉정을 유지하다

_“Even under pressure, I try to keep a cool head and focus on finding a practical solution.”_

**- How do you deal with a difficult coworker?**
talk over → 논의하다
sort out → 해결하다
conflict → 갈등
empathy → 공감력
agree to disagree → 의견 차이를 인정하다

_“When conflicts arise, I try to agree to disagree and focus on the shared goal.”_


3️⃣ **Goals & Motivation**

**- What are your short-term and long-term goals?**
aim for → 목표로 하다
build toward → 향해 나아가다
ambition → 야망
progression → 발전
set the bar high → 높은 목표를 세우다

_“I set the bar high by aiming to become a team leader within three years while developing leadership skills.”_

**- What motivates you to do your best at work?**
draw from → ~에서 얻다
push forward → 계속 나아가다
fulfillment → 성취감
challenge → 도전
drive me forward → 나를 앞으로 나아가게 하다

_“Solving complex problems drives me forward because it gives me a strong sense of fulfillment.”_

**- What kind of work environment helps you perform best?**
depend on → ~에 달려 있다
adapt to → 적응하다
autonomy → 자율성
atmosphere → 분위기
bring out the best in me → 나의 잠재력을 끌어내다

_“A collaborative environment brings out the best in me because I value open communication.”_

**- What does success mean to you?**
live up to → 기대에 부응하다
move toward → 향해 나아가다
satisfaction → 만족
integrity → 진정성
measure of success → 성공의 척도

_“For me, the true measure of success is growth that aligns with my personal values.”_

▪️ highlight your strengths
: 자신의 강점을 강조하다
“She highlighted her strengths during the interview.”
그녀는 면접에서 자신의 강점을 강조했습니다.

▪️ admit your weaknesses
: 자신의 약점을 솔직하게 인정하다
“It’s okay to admit your weaknesses if you show how you’re improving.”
개선하려는 노력을 함께 말한다면 약점을 인정해도 괜찮습니다.

💡 Tip #1 – “strength” vs. “advantage” vs. “skill”
strength → 개인적 강점 (성격, 태도)
advantage → 비교 우위 (다른 사람보다 나은 점)
skill → 기술적 능력 (배워서 익힌 능력)

▪️ follow up
: 후속 연락을 하다
“Don’t forget to follow up with a thank-you email.”
감사 이메일을 보내는 걸 잊지 마세요.

▪️ break the ice
: 어색한 분위기를 깨다
“The interviewer made a joke to break the ice.”
면접관이 농담을 해서 분위기를 풀었습니다.

💡 Tip #2 – “get + p.p.” 구조 자주 쓰이는 표현
get hired → 고용되다
get promoted → 승진하다
get rejected → 불합격되다
get fired → 해고되다

▪️ ask insightful questions
: 통찰력 있는 질문을 하다
“He asked insightful questions that impressed the interviewer.”
그는 면접관에게 깊은 인상을 주는 통찰력 있는 질문을 했습니다.

▪️ leave a strong impression
: 강한 인상을 남기다
“She left a strong impression with her enthusiasm.”
그녀는 열정으로 강한 인상을 남겼습니다.

💡 Tip #3 – “nonverbal” communication
eye contact → 눈 맞춤
posture → 자세
gesture → 손짓, 몸짓
facial expression → 표정

items.snippet: 1️⃣ **Patience & Limits** **- How patient are you with slow people or situations?** patience → 인내심 delay → 지연 wait for → 기다리다 deal with → 대처하다 lose your cool →
items.body_raw:
1️⃣ **Patience & Limits**

**- How patient are you with slow people or situations?**
patience → 인내심
delay → 지연
wait for → 기다리다
deal with → 대처하다
lose your cool → 화를 내다, 침착함을 잃다

_“I try to stay calm in traffic, but when people keep cutting in line, it’s hard not to lose my cool.”_

**- What kinds of situations easily test your limits?**
frustration → 좌절
tolerance → 참을성
put up with → 참다
run out of → 다 떨어지다
push someone’s buttons → 짜증나게 하다

_“I’m usually patient, but constant noise or arguing really pushes my buttons after a while.”_

**- Do you think patience can be trained, like a skill?**
discipline → 훈련
habit → 습관
build up → 쌓다
work on → 향상시키다
practice makes perfect → 연습이 완벽을 만든다

_“I’ve been working on staying calm in stressful situations — practice makes perfect when it comes to patience.”_


2️⃣ **Challenges & Self-Tests**

**- Have you ever challenged yourself to break a bad habit or endure discomfort?**
determination → 결단력
willpower → 의지력
give up → 포기하다
get through → 버티다
mind over matter → 의지로 극복하다

_“When I quit caffeine, it was tough at first, but it reminded me that it’s all mind over matter.”_

**- What’s a ‘limit’ you’d like to push next — physical, mental, or emotional?**
resilience → 회복력
threshold → 한계점
push through → 끝까지 해내다
step out of → 벗어나다
out of your comfort zone → 익숙한 틀을 벗어난

_“I want to step out of my comfort zone and take on a marathon to test both my physical and mental limits.”_

**- How do you usually know when you’ve reached your limit?**
fatigue → 피로
breakdown → 붕괴, 한계점
slow down → 속도를 늦추다
burn out → 지치다
hit the wall → 한계에 부딪히다

_“When I start forgetting things and can’t focus, I know I’ve hit the wall and need a real break.”_


3️⃣ **Emotional & Social Limits**

**- How much socializing can you handle before needing alone time?**
energy → 에너지
introvert → 내향적인 사람
hang out → 어울리다
wind down → 진정하다, 쉬다
drain your energy → 에너지를 소모시키다

_“I love hanging out with friends, but big gatherings eventually drain my energy, so I need quiet time to recharge.”_

**- Are you easily affected by others’ moods or comments?**
sensitivity → 민감함
influence → 영향
pick up on → 눈치채다
shut out → 차단하다
get under your skin → 신경을 건드리다

_“I try not to take things personally, but sometimes negative energy from others really gets under my skin.”_

**- How do you handle criticism or rejection?**
feedback → 피드백
confidence → 자신감
learn from → 배우다
move on → 잊고 나아가다
take it with a grain of salt → 가볍게 받아들이다

_“I try to take criticism with a grain of salt — listen to what’s useful, then move on without dwelling on it.”_

▪️ test someone’s patience
: 누군가의 인내심을 시험하다
“The constant noise really tested my patience.”
계속되는 소음이 정말 제 인내심을 시험했습니다.

▪️ grin and bear it
: (불쾌하지만) 웃으며 참다
“I didn’t like the task, but I had to grin and bear it.”
그 일이 마음에 들지 않았지만 웃으며 참아야 했습니다.

💡 Tip #1 – “bear”의 핵심 의미
bear → 참다, 견디다
can’t bear → 도저히 못 참다
bear responsibility → 책임을 지다

▪️ bite your tongue
: 하고 싶은 말을 참다
“I had to bite my tongue not to argue back.”
되받아치고 싶었지만 말을 참아야 했습니다.

▪️ be on edge
: 신경이 곤두서 있다
“I’ve been on edge all week because of the deadline.”
마감일 때문에 일주일 내내 신경이 곤두서 있었습니다.

💡 Tip #2 – “It drives me crazy!” vs. “It stresses me out!”
drives me crazy → 짜증나거나 너무 귀찮을 때
“The noise drives me crazy.” (소음이 짜증 나요.)
stresses me out → 부담감, 압박감 때문에 힘들 때
“Deadlines stress me out.” (마감이 부담돼요.)

▪️ lose control
: 감정을 통제하지 못하다
“He lost control and shouted at everyone.”
그는 감정을 참지 못하고 모두에게 소리쳤습니다.

▪️ set boundaries
: 한계를 정하다, 선을 긋다
“It’s important to set boundaries for your own peace.”
자신의 평화를 위해 선을 긋는 것이 중요합니다.

💡 Tip #3 – “boundary” 자주 쓰이는 표현
personal boundary → 개인적 한계
emotional boundary → 감정적 거리
cross the line → 선을 넘다
